hypothesis: If a diffusion model with explicit stereochemical graph encodings (LoQI) is trained on the AIMNet2-relaxed ChEMBL3D dataset, then it will scalably generate topologically correct, stereochemically accurate conformers that are lower in energy and closer to DFT minima than standard conformer generators across diverse drug-like molecules because the stereochemical constraints and equivariant architecture guide sampling onto the physically valid low-energy manifold.
